description: Schema and fixture corpus for recording exact validation frontiers when broad proof commands stop in unrelated blockers.

decision_classes.meaning: The intended proof command completed and is the primary validation claim.
The intended proof command or commit path stopped in an unrelated external blocker; cite the blocker and the narrower supplemental proof.
The intended proof command stopped in a blocker caused by the owned slice itself.

error_classes.ownership_expectation: Route to the file owner or local bead when the touched slice caused the compile stop.
Treat unrelated lint debt as an external blocker unless the touched slice introduced it.
Treat as external coordination blocker; cite the holding agent and expiry.
Treat as external shared-main blocker; cite the staged peer paths before committing.

record_fields.name: command
record_fields.description: The intended proof or coordination command that was attempted.

record_fields.name: touched_files
record_fields.description: Owned files that motivated the validation attempt.

record_fields.name: decision
record_fields.description: How the result should be cited in a close reason.

record_fields.name: first_failure.crate_or_surface
record_fields.description: The first crate or coordination surface that stopped the attempt.

record_fields.name: first_failure.target
record_fields.description: The first failing cargo target or coordination target.

record_fields.name: first_failure.file
record_fields.description: The first failing file or path.

record_fields.name: first_failure.line
record_fields.description: Line number for code blockers; 0 for coordination-only blockers.

record_fields.name: likely_owner
record_fields.description: Best current ownership hint, including 'local_change' or external agent names.

record_fields.name: likely_bead
record_fields.description: Best current bead hint when known.

record_fields.name: supplemental_proof_command
record_fields.description: The narrower proof that still covered the local change.

record_fields.name: summary
record_fields.description: Short blocker summary that can be pasted directly into a close reason.

close_reason_template.example: blocked-external: intended `rch exec -- cargo test --test combinator_select_fairness_determinism_audit -- --nocapture`; stopped at `src/sync/semaphore.rs:37` (`rustc_compile_error`, unused imports) while touching `tests/combinator_select_fairness_determinism_audit.rs`; supplemental proof `rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check tests/combinator_select_fairness_determinism_audit.rs`.

redaction_rules: Do not include home-directory paths or bearer tokens.
Prefer repo-relative file paths and exact rustc/clippy error summaries.
Record local fallback markers explicitly instead of hiding them.

fixtures.snippet:
error[E0004]: non-exhaustive patterns: `DeliverPolicy::ByStartTime(_)` not covered
  --> src/messaging/consumer.rs:2198:11
   |
2198 |     match deliver_policy {
   |           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ pattern `DeliverPolicy::ByStartTime(_)` not covered
   |
error: could not compile `asupersync` (lib) due to 1 previous error

fixtures.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p asupersync-conformance --bin otel_resource_conformance -- -D warnings
fixtures.supplemental_proof_command: rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync-conformance --bin otel_resource_conformance -- --nocapture
fixtures.snippet:

   --> src/observability/otel.rs:114:1
    |
114 | fn build_pipeline(...) {
    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    |
    = help: for further information visit https://rust-lang.github.io/rust-clippy/master/index.html#too_many_arguments
    = note: `-D clippy::too-many-arguments` implied by `-D warnings`
error: could not compile `asupersync` (lib) due to 1 previous error

expected_record.summary: this function has too many arguments (8/7)
exclusive reservation held by SunnyHollow until 2026-05-07T09:36:30.647793Z
unrelated staged paths present before commit
